hypothesis: Incorporating a confidence estimation head into E2EFOLD's decoder that predicts per-residue prediction uncertainty will reveal that the correlation between E2EFOLD and Boltz-2 prediction errors (Spearman's ρ = 0.60) is driven primarily by structurally ambiguous regions, and that using this uncertainty signal as a dynamic loss weight during end-to-end training—downweighting high-uncertainty residues in the structure recovery objective—will improve self-consistency TM-scores by selectively pressuring the encoder to avoid designing sequences that encode structurally ambiguous backbone regions.
